quote.text: Uneasy lies the head that wears a crown.
quote.author: William Shakespeare, Henry IV, Part 2 (1597)

stories.headline: Trump names the US "Guardian of the Strait," sets a 20% Hormuz toll — and finds no takers
stories.dek: A third weekend of strikes gave way Monday to a unilateral American toll on the world's busiest oil chokepoint. The Gulf states, the IMO and Beijing all said no before the gate even came down.

stories.kicker: The Macro Desk · Market Explanation, Not Advice
stories.headline: Today's inflation print measures a June that's already out of date
stories.dek: US CPI and five banks' Q2 earnings land the same morning the Fed's own rate-hike odds have cooled — just as a fresh oil shock starts pricing a July the data hasn't caught up to yet.

stories.headline: A snowman-shaped asteroid, and a checklist for the coldest "stars" in the galaxy
stories.dek: Japan's Hayabusa2 delivers a close-up of a contact binary 100 million kilometres out, while a new study sets observable criteria for spotting a Dyson sphere — without claiming to have found one.

stories.headline: The number lands at 8am — and so, likely, does the haze
stories.dek: MTI's advance Q2 GDP estimate is due this morning, with economists split on whether growth held near Q1's pace or eased toward it. Separately, the El Niño season now carries a "red" risk rating for transboundary haze.

stories.headline: Jurong Lake District's white site goes to tender — the biggest supply move of the second half
stories.dek: Nine Confirmed List sites join the 2H2026 government land sales programme, headlined by a mixed-use white site that could reshape Singapore's second CBD.

stories.headline: DeepSeek starts building its own chip, and finally sets a date for V4
stories.dek: A year-old, quietly-run effort to cut loose from Nvidia and Huawei surfaces the same week the model it's meant to serve gets a release date.

stories.headline: Samsung locks in 22 July — and the leaked Fold shape is a real departure
stories.dek: Unpacked is officially on the calendar, with Samsung signalling a foldable that looks nothing like the last four generations.

stories.headline: Apple teaches its cameras to describe what they see; Home Assistant teaches its network to explain itself
stories.dek: The same July brought the two ecosystems' biggest updates in years — and they solved for almost opposite problems.

stories.headline: Sennheiser's new flagship makes its battery a spare part, not a warranty claim
stories.dek: Two premium-audio houses mark this July differently — one with a genuine spec jump, the other with an anniversary edition of a proven design.

stories.headline: The Odyssey's tracking jumps past Oppenheimer's opening — a day before anyone's allowed to review it
stories.dek: Industry forecasters have revised Nolan's opening-weekend estimate sharply upward since the first reactions landed. The formal review embargo lifts tomorrow.

stories.headline: Chatbot-safety laws reach the states — 78 bills, 27 legislatures, and counting
stories.dek: Oregon became the latest state to pass a chatbot-safety bill this week, part of a wave built almost entirely around one specific risk: AI "companion" apps designed to feel like a relationship.

stories.headline: The "dating recession": most young adults who want marriage aren't dating at all
stories.dek: A large, nationally representative US survey finds low dating activity paired with low resilience — most who want serious relationships still say a single bad experience is enough to make them stop trying.

stories.headline: Ninety to 120 minutes a week — the strength-training sweet spot, from 30 years of data
stories.dek: A large new dataset finds a mortality-risk floor for resistance training, and it isn't "more is always better." General guidance, not medical advice.

stories.headline: ENHYPEN's on-sale finally opens tomorrow — BIGBANG's still hasn't, for an eighth straight issue
stories.dek: One long-running K-pop chase resolves on schedule; the other, dated and confirmed since No. 27, is still waiting on the one detail that actually lets a fan buy a ticket.

stories.headline: The Sound and the Strait
stories.dek: On tolls nobody else signed off on, crowns nobody else conferred, and the much duller discipline — measurement — that eventually settles both arguments.
